metadata.disclaimer: Based on expert analysis mapping AICM v1.0.3 control objectives to NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls. AICM extends CSA CCM v4 with 46 AI-specific controls across 18 domains including the new Model Security (MDS) domain. Coverage percentages reflect NIST addressing of cloud and AI security intent. Validate with qualified assessors for compliance/audit use.

clauses.title: Audit and Assurance Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: CA-01 establishes assessment policy and procedures; CA-02 defines control assessments; AU-01 covers audit and accountability policy. Together these address the policy and procedural foundations for audit and assurance.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific audit policies covering model governance, algorithmic accountability, and AI system lifecycle audit trails that NIST does not explicitly address.

clauses.title: Independent Assessments
clauses.rationale: CA-02 requires independent assessments of security controls; CA-07 provides continuous monitoring; CA-08 covers penetration testing.
clauses.gaps: AICM emphasizes independent assessment of AI systems including model validation, bias auditing, and algorithmic impact assessments not covered by NIST.

clauses.title: Risk Based Planning Assessment
clauses.rationale: RA-03 covers risk assessment; CA-02 addresses control assessment planning; RA-07 covers risk response.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires risk-based assessment planning that accounts for AI-specific risks (model drift, adversarial attacks, training data bias) beyond traditional IT risk.

clauses.title: Requirements Compliance
clauses.rationale: CA-02 assesses control compliance; CA-05 produces plans of action and milestones; CA-09 covers internal system connections.
clauses.gaps: AICM focuses on demonstrating compliance with AI-specific regulations (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF) and cloud-specific evidence requirements.

clauses.title: Audit Management Process
clauses.rationale: CA-02 manages security assessments; CA-05 tracks remediation via POA&M; AU-06 covers audit review, analysis, and reporting.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires structured audit management including AI model audit trails, training data provenance audits, and algorithmic decision logging.

clauses.title: Remediation
clauses.rationale: CA-05 establishes remediation plans (POA&M); CA-02 provides assessment framework; RA-07 addresses risk response.
clauses.gaps: AICM specifies AI-specific remediation including model retraining schedules, bias correction plans, and AI incident response procedures.

clauses.title: Application and Interface Security Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: SA-01 establishes system acquisition policy; SA-08 covers security engineering principles; SI-01 provides system and information integrity policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI application security policies including ML pipeline security, model serving infrastructure, and AI API protection requirements.

clauses.title: Application Security Baseline Requirements
clauses.rationale: SA-08 defines security engineering principles; SA-11 covers developer testing and evaluation; SA-15 addresses development process and standards.
clauses.gaps: AICM baseline requirements extend to AI-specific secure development practices including adversarial robustness, model integrity verification, and ML framework security.

clauses.title: Application Security Metrics
clauses.rationale: CA-07 provides continuous monitoring; PM-06 covers security metrics; SA-11 addresses developer security testing.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific application security metrics including model performance degradation monitoring, drift detection, and adversarial input detection.

clauses.title: Secure Application Development Lifecycle
clauses.rationale: SA-03 covers system development lifecycle; SA-08 addresses security engineering; SA-11 covers developer testing; SA-15 covers development standards.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends SDLC to MLOps lifecycle including model training, validation, deployment, and monitoring phases with AI-specific security gates.

clauses.title: Application Security Testing
clauses.rationale: CA-08 covers penetration testing; RA-05 addresses vulnerability scanning; SA-11 covers developer security testing.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific security testing including adversarial robustness testing, model extraction attacks, and training data poisoning assessments.

clauses.title: Secure Application Deployment
clauses.rationale: CM-02 provides baseline configurations; CM-03 covers configuration change control; SA-03 addresses system development lifecycle.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses automated secure deployment pipelines for ML models including model versioning, rollback capabilities, and deployment validation.

clauses.title: Application Vulnerability Remediation
clauses.rationale: RA-05 covers vulnerability monitoring and scanning; SA-11 addresses developer security testing and evaluation.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific vulnerability assessment including model vulnerability scanning, dependency analysis for ML frameworks, and AI supply chain security.

clauses.title: Input Validation
clauses.rationale: SA-08 covers security engineering principles; SC-07 provides boundary protection; AC-04 addresses information flow enforcement for API security.
clauses.gaps: AICM AIS-08 specifically addresses AI API security including model inference endpoint protection, rate limiting for AI services, and input validation for model queries. NIST lacks AI API-specific controls.

clauses.title: Output Validation
clauses.rationale: SA-11 covers developer testing; SI-07 addresses software integrity; CM-03 covers change control. These partially address AI model integrity.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI model integrity verification including cryptographic model signing, tamper detection for model weights, and inference integrity validation.

clauses.title: API Security
clauses.rationale: SA-08/SA-11 cover security engineering and testing; RA-05 covers vulnerability scanning. These provide a general framework but lack AI specificity.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses adversarial robustness requirements including adversarial input detection, model hardening against evasion attacks, and robustness testing methodologies.

clauses.title: Agents Security Boundaries
clauses.rationale: SA-03 covers development lifecycle; SA-15 addresses development standards; CM-03 covers change control for ML pipeline security.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires ML pipeline security controls including secure training infrastructure, feature store protection, and automated ML security gates.

clauses.title: Source Code Managemement
clauses.rationale: SI-04 covers information system monitoring; CA-07 provides continuous monitoring; AU-06 covers audit review and analysis.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses AI-specific runtime monitoring including model behavior anomaly detection, inference drift monitoring, and automated model rollback triggers.

clauses.title: AI Sandboxing
clauses.rationale: SA-11 covers developer testing; CA-08 addresses penetration testing; SI-07 covers software integrity verification.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI red teaming including adversarial ML attacks, prompt injection testing, model extraction attempts, and AI-specific threat simulation.

clauses.title: AI Cache Protection
clauses.rationale: SA-08 covers security engineering; SC-13 addresses cryptographic protection; SI-07 covers software integrity.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses model provenance and supply chain security including model origin verification, training data lineage, and third-party model validation.

clauses.title: Prompt Differentation
clauses.rationale: SA-03/SA-08/SA-11 provide general software security lifecycle controls applicable to AI development.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires responsible AI development practices including fairness testing, explainability requirements, and human oversight integration. NIST 800-53 does not address responsible AI.

clauses.title: Business Continuity Management Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: CP-01 establishes contingency planning policy; CP-02 defines contingency plans.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends BCR to AI system resilience including model failover, graceful degradation of AI services, and fallback to non-AI decision paths.

clauses.title: Risk Assessment and Impact Analysis
clauses.rationale: CP-02 covers contingency planning; RA-03 addresses risk assessment; RA-09 covers criticality analysis.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific business impact analysis including model dependency mapping and AI service criticality assessment.

clauses.title: Business Continuity Strategy
clauses.rationale: CP-02/CP-07/CP-08 address contingency planning, alternate processing sites, and telecommunications services.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI-specific redundancy including model replication, distributed inference capabilities, and training infrastructure resilience.

clauses.title: Business Continuity Planning
clauses.rationale: CP-02 covers contingency planning; CP-03/CP-04 address testing and training for continuity.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI system continuity testing including model failover validation and AI service degradation drills.

clauses.title: Documentation
clauses.rationale: PL-02 covers security concept of operations; PL-07 covers security concept consistency; CP-02 addresses contingency planning.
clauses.gaps: AICM documentation requirements extend to AI model documentation, training data documentation, and AI system architecture records.

clauses.title: Business Continuity Exercises
clauses.rationale: CP-03 covers contingency plan testing; CP-04 addresses training for contingency roles.
clauses.gaps: AICM testing extends to AI-specific scenarios including model corruption recovery and training data restoration.

clauses.title: Communication
clauses.rationale: CP-02/CP-08 address contingency and telecommunications; IR-06 covers incident reporting.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses AI-specific communication plans during outages including model performance degradation notifications.

clauses.title: Backup
clauses.rationale: CP-06 covers alternate storage sites; CP-09 addresses information system backup.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends backup to AI artifacts including model checkpoints, training data snapshots, and feature store backups.

clauses.title: Disaster Response Plan
clauses.rationale: CP-02 addresses contingency planning; CP-10 covers system recovery; IR-01 establishes incident response policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific recovery including model restoration, training pipeline recovery, and AI service reconstitution procedures.

clauses.title: Response Plan Exercise
clauses.rationale: CP-04 covers contingency training; IR-03 addresses incident response testing.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires impact analysis specific to AI system failures including cascading model dependency impacts.

clauses.title: Equipment Redundancy
clauses.rationale: CP-07/CP-08 cover alternate processing and telecommunications; PE-11 addresses emergency power.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI infrastructure resilience including GPU cluster failover and distributed training resilience.

clauses.title: Change Management Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: CM-01 establishes configuration management policy; CM-03 covers change control; CM-09 addresses configuration management plans.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI model versioning, ML experiment tracking, and model configuration management.

clauses.title: Quality Testing
clauses.rationale: CM-03 covers change control; CM-04 addresses impact analysis; SA-11 covers developer testing.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific change impact analysis including model performance regression testing and bias impact assessment.

clauses.title: Change Management Technology
clauses.rationale: CM-03 covers change control processes; CM-05 addresses access restrictions; CM-09 covers configuration plans.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends change control to AI model deployment pipelines and training data change management.

clauses.title: Change Authorization
clauses.rationale: CM-05 restricts changes to configuration items; SI-07 provides software and information integrity verification.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model integrity verification during deployment and runtime integrity monitoring.

clauses.title: Change Agreements
clauses.rationale: SA-04 covers acquisition processes; CM-03 addresses change control.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses change control for third-party AI models and pre-trained model integration.

clauses.title: Change Management Baseline
clauses.rationale: CM-02 establishes baseline configurations; CM-06 covers configuration settings enforcement.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI infrastructure baseline configurations including GPU cluster settings and model serving configurations.

clauses.title: Detection of Baseline Deviation
clauses.rationale: CM-02/CM-03 cover baselines and change control; SI-07 provides integrity verification.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires detection of unauthorized changes to AI models, training data, and inference pipelines.

clauses.title: Exception Management
clauses.rationale: CA-05 tracks remediation via POA&M; PM-09 covers risk management strategy; PL-02 covers security concept of operations.
clauses.gaps: AICM technology risk management extends to AI technology risk including model risk, data risk, and algorithmic risk.

clauses.title: Change Restoration
clauses.rationale: CP-09 covers backup; CP-10 addresses recovery; CM-03 covers change control for rollback procedures.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific rollback including model version rollback, training data rollback, and feature store restoration.

clauses.title: Encryption and Key Management Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: SC-01 establishes system and communications protection policy; SC-12 covers key establishment; SC-13 addresses cryptographic protection.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to encryption for AI training data, model weights, and inference data in transit/at rest.

clauses.title: CEK Roles and Responsibilities
clauses.rationale: PS-01 covers personnel security policy; PL-02 covers security concepts; SC-12 addresses key management roles.
clauses.gaps: AICM specifies key management roles for AI systems including model encryption key custodians.

clauses.title: Data Encryption
clauses.rationale: SC-08 covers transmission confidentiality; SC-13 addresses cryptographic protection; SC-28 covers protection at rest.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data encryption to AI-specific data including training datasets, model parameters, and inference results.

clauses.title: Encryption Algorithm
clauses.rationale: SC-13 directly addresses the use of approved cryptographic algorithms and mechanisms.
clauses.gaps: AICM may require emerging cryptographic standards for AI-specific use cases like homomorphic encryption for privacy-preserving ML.

clauses.title: Encryption Change Management
clauses.rationale: CM-03 covers change control; SC-13 addresses cryptographic mechanisms. Together they support encryption change management.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses cryptographic agility for AI systems including post-quantum readiness for model protection.

clauses.title: Encryption Change Cost Benefit Analysis
clauses.rationale: RA-03 covers risk assessment; SC-13 addresses cryptographic protection. Together they support risk-based encryption.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires risk assessment of cryptographic approaches for AI data including training data sensitivity classification.

clauses.title: Encryption Risk Management
clauses.rationale: RA-03/RA-07 cover risk assessment and response; SC-13 addresses cryptography.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI-specific risk assessment of cryptographic controls for model and data protection.

clauses.title: Customer Key Management Capability
clauses.rationale: SC-12 directly addresses cryptographic key establishment and management.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends key management to AI-specific scenarios including federated learning key distribution.

clauses.title: Encryption and Key Management Audit
clauses.rationale: AU-02 covers audit events; CA-02 addresses assessments; SC-12 covers key management.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires monitoring and auditing of cryptographic operations in AI pipelines.

clauses.title: Key Generation
clauses.rationale: SC-12/SC-13 comprehensively cover key management and cryptographic protection.
clauses.gaps: AICM specifies key generation requirements for AI-specific encryption including hardware security module integration.

clauses.title: Key Purpose
clauses.rationale: SC-12 addresses key establishment and management including key distribution.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to secure key distribution for distributed AI systems and federated learning.

clauses.title: Key Rotation
clauses.rationale: SC-12 addresses key management including key access and authorization.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends key access controls to AI pipeline components and model serving infrastructure.

clauses.title: Key Revocation
clauses.rationale: SC-12 covers key management; SC-17 addresses public key infrastructure certificates.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends certificate management to AI service endpoints and model signing certificates.

clauses.title: Key Destruction
clauses.rationale: MP-06 covers media sanitization; SC-12 addresses key management including destruction.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends key destruction to AI-specific contexts including model decommissioning and training data deletion keys.

clauses.title: Key Activation
clauses.rationale: SC-12 comprehensively covers key lifecycle management including activation and suspension.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends key activation/deactivation to AI model lifecycle events.

clauses.title: Key Suspension
clauses.rationale: SC-12 covers key management including key recovery mechanisms.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends key recovery to AI-specific disaster recovery scenarios.

clauses.title: Key Deactivation
clauses.rationale: SC-12 covers key management including key escrow where applicable.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses AI-specific key escrow requirements for regulated AI systems.

clauses.title: Key Archival
clauses.rationale: CP-09 covers backup; SC-12 addresses key management including key archiving.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends key archiving to long-term AI model preservation and reproducibility requirements.

clauses.title: Key Compromise
clauses.rationale: IR-01/IR-06 cover incident response; SC-12 addresses key management during security incidents.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses key compromise response in AI contexts including model re-encryption procedures.

clauses.title: Key Recovery
clauses.rationale: CP-09 covers backup; SC-12 addresses key management including key backup procedures.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends key backup to AI-specific infrastructure including training cluster key management.

clauses.title: Key Inventory Management
clauses.rationale: CM-08 covers information system component inventory; SC-12 addresses key management inventory.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires comprehensive key inventory for AI systems including model encryption keys and data protection keys.

clauses.title: Off-Site Equipment Disposal Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: MP-06 covers media sanitization; PE-01 addresses physical access authorizations.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to security of AI-specific hardware including GPU clusters and specialized AI accelerators.

clauses.title: Off-Site Transfer Authorization Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: MP-05 covers media transport; PE-01/PE-16 address physical access and delivery/removal.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses secure handling of AI hardware and specialized computing equipment.

clauses.title: Secure Area Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: PE-01/PE-02/PE-03 comprehensively cover physical access authorization, enforcement, and monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends physical access controls to AI training facilities and GPU datacenters.

clauses.title: Secure Media Transportation Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: MP-01 establishes media protection policy; MP-05 covers media transport.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses handling of AI-specific media including training data storage and model weight archives.

clauses.title: Assets Classification
clauses.rationale: CM-08 covers asset inventory; MP-04 addresses media storage; RA-02 covers security categorization.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends asset classification to AI-specific assets including training data, models, and inference endpoints.

clauses.title: Assets Cataloguing and Tracking
clauses.rationale: CM-08 covers component inventory; PE-05 addresses physical access monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends asset management to AI infrastructure lifecycle including GPU deprecation and AI hardware refresh.

clauses.title: Controlled Physical Access Points
clauses.rationale: PE-03 covers physical access monitoring; PE-06 addresses visitor management.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends visitor controls to sensitive AI facilities.

clauses.title: Equipment Identification
clauses.rationale: CM-08 covers component inventory; IA-03 addresses device identification and authentication.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends device management to AI training nodes and inference hardware.

clauses.title: Secure Area Authorization
clauses.rationale: PE-02/PE-03 cover physical access control and monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to access control for AI compute facilities.

clauses.title: Surveillance System
clauses.rationale: PE-06 covers monitoring; PE-08 addresses visitor access records.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends surveillance to AI-specific facilities.

clauses.title: Adverse Event Response Training
clauses.rationale: AT-03 covers security training; IR-02 addresses incident response training; PE-06 covers monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI facility-specific security awareness including handling of sensitive training data.

clauses.title: Cabling Security
clauses.rationale: PE-04 covers transmission medium access control; PE-09 addresses power and cabling.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends cabling security to high-bandwidth AI training interconnects.

clauses.title: Environmental Systems
clauses.rationale: PE-13/PE-14/PE-15 comprehensively cover fire, temperature/humidity, and water damage protection.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends environmental controls to high-density AI compute environments with specific cooling requirements.

clauses.title: Secure Utilities
clauses.rationale: PE-09 covers power equipment; PE-10 addresses emergency shutoff; PE-11 covers emergency power.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends power management to high-power AI training clusters.

clauses.title: Equipment Location
clauses.rationale: PE-05 covers access control for output devices; PE-18 addresses location of information system components.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends equipment location considerations to AI infrastructure placement and edge AI deployment.

clauses.title: Security and Privacy Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: AC-01 establishes access control policy; PL-01 covers planning policy; PT-01 addresses personally identifiable information processing policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI-specific data security policies including training data governance, model data handling, and AI output data protection.

clauses.title: Secure Disposal
clauses.rationale: MP-06 covers media sanitization; SI-12 addresses information management and retention.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data handling to AI training data lifecycle, model artifact management, and synthetic data governance.

clauses.title: Data Inventory
clauses.rationale: CM-08 covers inventory; PM-05 addresses information security workforce; PT-03 covers data minimization.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific data inventory including training datasets, feature stores, and model registries.

clauses.title: Data Classification
clauses.rationale: AC-16 covers security and privacy attributes; RA-02 addresses security categorization.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data classification to AI data types including training data sensitivity, model weight classification, and inference data categorization.

clauses.title: Data Flow Documentation
clauses.rationale: AC-04 covers information flow enforcement; CA-09 addresses internal connections; PL-02 covers security concepts.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data flow mapping to AI pipelines including training data flows, model serving paths, and feedback loops.

clauses.title: Data Ownership and Stewardship
clauses.rationale: AC-16 covers security attributes; PM-05 addresses workforce; PT-01 covers PII processing.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires data ownership for AI systems including training data provenance, model IP ownership, and synthetic data rights.

clauses.title: Data Protection by Design and Default
clauses.rationale: SA-08 covers security engineering; SC-28 provides protection at rest; PT-01 addresses PII processing policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends privacy by design to AI systems including privacy-preserving ML techniques, differential privacy, and federated learning.

clauses.title: Data Privacy by Design and Default
clauses.rationale: PT-01/PT-02/PT-03 address PII processing, authority, and data minimization.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends personal data handling to AI-specific scenarios including automated profiling, AI-driven decision making, and training data containing PII.

clauses.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment
clauses.rationale: PT-01 covers PII processing; RA-03/RA-08 address risk assessment and privacy impact.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific data protection impact assessments including algorithmic impact assessments and AI bias assessments.

clauses.title: Sensitive Data Transfer
clauses.rationale: AC-04 enforces information flow; SC-08 provides transmission confidentiality; SC-13 covers cryptographic protection.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data transfer controls to AI-specific transfers including model weight distribution and federated learning communications.

clauses.title: Personal Data Access, Reversal, Rectification and Deletion
clauses.rationale: PT-04/PT-05/PT-06 cover individual consent, privacy notice, and system of records.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends consent management to AI-specific scenarios including consent for automated decision-making and AI training data usage.

clauses.title: Limitation of Purpose in Personal Data Processing
clauses.rationale: PT-02 covers authority for PII processing; PT-03 addresses data minimization and retention.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data purpose limitation to AI including training data reuse restrictions, model purpose limitations, and transfer learning constraints.

clauses.title: Personal Data Sub-processing
clauses.rationale: PT-01 covers PII processing; SA-04/SA-09 address acquisition and external systems.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to third-party AI data processing including cloud AI services, third-party model APIs, and data annotation services.

clauses.title: Disclosure of Data Sub-processors
clauses.rationale: PT-01 covers PII processing policy; SA-09 addresses external information system services.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses cross-border AI data transfer restrictions including model training across jurisdictions and AI service localization requirements.

clauses.title: Limitation of Production Data Use
clauses.rationale: CM-04 covers security impact analysis; PT-03 addresses data minimization.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data retention to AI including training data retention, model artifact retention, and experiment metadata preservation.

clauses.title: Data Retention and Deletion
clauses.rationale: MP-06 covers sanitization; PT-01 addresses PII processing; SI-12 covers information management.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends secure disposal to AI artifacts including model deletion, training data sanitization, and inference log purging.

clauses.title: Sensitive Data Protection
clauses.rationale: AC-03 covers access enforcement; SC-08/SC-28 address data protection in transit and at rest.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data protection to AI-specific data including model weights, embeddings, and training data encryption.

clauses.title: Disclosure Notification
clauses.rationale: IR-06 covers incident reporting; PT-01 addresses PII processing policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends breach notification to AI-specific incidents including model compromise, training data exposure, and adversarial attack disclosure.

clauses.title: Data Location
clauses.rationale: PT-01 covers PII processing; SA-09 addresses external information system services.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends subprocessor management to AI-specific scenarios including third-party model providers and data annotation subcontractors.

clauses.title: Data Provenance and Transparency
clauses.rationale: PT-01/PT-03 cover PII processing and minimization; SA-08 covers security engineering principles.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses training data governance including data provenance, consent for training use, data quality requirements, and bias assessment in training datasets. NIST lacks AI training data-specific controls.

clauses.title: Data Poisoning Prevention & Detection
clauses.rationale: PT-01 covers PII processing; SI-12 addresses information management; RA-03 covers risk assessment.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses synthetic data governance including generation controls, privacy guarantees, utility validation, and synthetic data lineage tracking.

clauses.title: Privacy Enhancing Technologies
clauses.rationale: AC-04 covers information flow; PT-01 addresses PII processing; SC-13 covers cryptographic protection for privacy-preserving computation.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses privacy-preserving machine learning techniques including differential privacy, federated learning governance, and secure multi-party computation for AI. NIST does not address these AI-specific privacy techniques.

clauses.title: Data Integrity Check
clauses.rationale: PT-01/PT-04 cover PII processing and consent; SA-09 addresses external services.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses AI output data governance including generated content attribution, deepfake detection requirements, and AI-generated data labeling obligations.

clauses.title: Data Differentiation and Relevance
clauses.rationale: SI-12 covers information management; PT-03 addresses data minimization; AU-02 covers audit events.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses model data lineage requirements including end-to-end traceability from training data through model artifacts to inference outputs. NIST does not address AI data lineage.

clauses.title: Governance Program Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: PL-01/PM-01/PM-02 establish security planning, program, and roles.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends governance to AI-specific oversight including AI ethics boards, model governance committees, and algorithmic accountability structures.

clauses.title: Risk Management Program
clauses.rationale: PM-09 covers risk management strategy; RA-01/RA-03 establish risk assessment policy and process.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends risk management to AI-specific risks including model risk, algorithmic bias, and AI system safety.

clauses.title: Organizational Policy Reviews
clauses.rationale: PL-01 covers planning policy; PM-01 establishes information security program.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific security program elements including AI governance frameworks and responsible AI programs.

clauses.title: Policy Exception Process
clauses.rationale: CA-05 covers plans of action; PL-02 addresses security concept of operations.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI governance roadmaps and AI security program maturity tracking.

clauses.title: Information Security Program
clauses.rationale: PM-01/PM-02/PM-03 cover security program, roles, and capital planning.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI-specific resource allocation including AI security budget, AI talent requirements, and AI infrastructure investment.

clauses.title: Governance Responsibility Model
clauses.rationale: PL-02 covers security concepts; PM-01/PM-02 establish program and roles.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends security program to AI-specific components including AI risk management frameworks and AI security operations.

clauses.title: Information System Regulatory Mapping
clauses.rationale: CA-02 covers assessments; PM-01 establishes program; PL-02 covers security concepts.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific policy review cycles including updates for emerging AI threats and regulatory changes.

clauses.title: Special Interest Groups
clauses.rationale: PM-15 covers security groups and associations; PM-16 addresses threat awareness program.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends stakeholder engagement to AI-specific parties including AI researchers, ethics boards, and AI regulators.

clauses.title: Acceptable Use of the AI Service
clauses.rationale: PM-01/PM-09 cover security program and risk strategy; RA-01 establishes risk assessment policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses AI governance frameworks including responsible AI principles, AI ethics policies, and organizational AI strategy alignment. NIST 800-53 does not address AI governance specifically.

clauses.title: AI Impact Assessment
clauses.rationale: PM-01/PM-02 establish security program and roles; PM-09 covers risk management strategy.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI ethics oversight including ethics review boards, algorithmic impact assessments, and human rights due diligence for AI systems.

clauses.title: Bias and Fairness Assessment
clauses.rationale: RA-01/RA-03 cover risk assessment; PM-09 addresses risk management strategy.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses AI-specific risk management including model risk quantification, AI system risk taxonomy, and AI risk appetite definition.

clauses.title: Ethics Committee
clauses.rationale: PM-01 establishes program; CA-02/CA-07 cover assessment and monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses AI regulatory compliance monitoring including tracking of emerging AI regulations (EU AI Act, state AI laws) and proactive compliance adaptation.

clauses.title: Explainability Requirement
clauses.rationale: PM-02 covers roles; PS-01 addresses personnel security; AT-01 covers training policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific roles and responsibilities including AI safety officers, model validators, and AI ethics leads.

clauses.title: Explainability Evaluation
clauses.rationale: PM-01/PM-09/RA-03 provide general governance and risk management frameworks.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses responsible AI principles including fairness, accountability, transparency, and explainability (FATE) requirements not covered by NIST 800-53.

clauses.title: Human supervision
clauses.rationale: PM-15/PM-16 cover external engagement and threat awareness; CA-07 covers continuous monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI incident reporting to regulators and stakeholders including mandatory disclosure of AI system failures and bias incidents.

clauses.title: Background Screening Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: PS-01 establishes personnel security policy; PS-03 covers personnel screening.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends screening to AI-specific roles including data scientists, ML engineers, and AI ethics reviewers.

clauses.title: Acceptable Use of Technology Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: AC-20 covers use of external systems; PL-04 addresses rules of behavior.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends acceptable use to AI systems including acceptable use of AI tools, model usage policies, and AI output verification requirements.

clauses.title: Clean Desk Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: AC-11 covers session lock; MP-02 addresses media access.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends clean desk to AI workstation security including training data access and model development environment protection.

clauses.title: Remote and Home Working Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: AC-17 covers remote access; PE-17 addresses alternate work site security.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends remote work security to AI development including remote access to training infrastructure and model repositories.

clauses.title: Asset returns
clauses.rationale: PS-04 covers personnel termination procedures.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends termination to AI-specific access revocation including model repositories, training infrastructure, and AI service accounts.

clauses.title: Employment Termination
clauses.rationale: PS-04/PS-05 cover personnel termination and transfer procedures.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends role change procedures to AI-specific access including model governance roles and data access permissions.

clauses.title: Employment Agreement Process
clauses.rationale: PS-01 covers personnel security policy; PS-06 addresses access agreements.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends non-disclosure to AI-specific intellectual property including model architectures, training methodologies, and AI research.

clauses.title: Employment Agreement Content
clauses.rationale: PL-04 covers rules of behavior; PS-06 addresses access agreements.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends rules of behavior to AI-specific requirements including responsible AI use and AI ethics obligations.

clauses.title: Personnel Roles and Responsibilities
clauses.rationale: PL-02 covers security concepts; PM-02 addresses roles; PS-01 covers personnel security.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends security roles to AI-specific positions and responsibilities.

clauses.title: Non-Disclosure Agreements
clauses.rationale: PS-06 covers access agreements; PS-09 addresses position categorization.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends compliance monitoring to AI-specific obligations and ethical commitments.

clauses.title: Security Awareness Training
clauses.rationale: AT-01/AT-02/AT-03 comprehensively cover security awareness training policy, content, and role-based training.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends security training to AI-specific topics including adversarial ML awareness, AI ethics training, and responsible AI use.

clauses.title: Personal and Sensitive Data Awareness and Training
clauses.rationale: AT-02/AT-03 cover security awareness and role-based training; PT-01 addresses PII processing.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends personal data training to AI-specific scenarios including training data handling, AI privacy risks, and automated decision-making implications.

clauses.title: Compliance User Responsibility
clauses.rationale: AT-02 covers awareness training; PL-04/PS-06 address rules of behavior and agreements.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI ethics and responsible use training.

clauses.title: AI Competency Training
clauses.rationale: AT-02/AT-03 cover security awareness and role-based training; PM-02 addresses roles.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific competency development including ML security skills, adversarial ML knowledge, and AI safety training programs. NIST does not address AI competency frameworks.

clauses.title: AI Acceptable Use
clauses.rationale: AT-01 covers training policy; PM-02 addresses roles; PS-01 covers personnel security.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI ethics training and certification including responsible AI practices, bias awareness, and AI governance education.

clauses.title: Identity and Access Management Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: AC-01 establishes access control policy; IA-01 covers identification and authentication policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends IAM policies to AI systems including model access control, training infrastructure authentication, and AI API authorization.

clauses.title: Strong Password Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: IA-01/IA-05 cover identification/authentication policy and authenticator management.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends identity management to AI-specific identities including service accounts for AI pipelines and model signing identities.

clauses.title: Identity Inventory
clauses.rationale: AC-02 covers account management; IA-04 addresses identifier management.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends account management to AI system accounts including training job identities and inference service accounts.

clauses.title: Separation of Duties
clauses.rationale: AC-05 covers separation of duties; AC-06 addresses least privilege.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends separation of duties to AI roles including model development, validation, and deployment role separation.

clauses.title: Least Privilege
clauses.rationale: AC-02 covers account management; AC-06 addresses least privilege.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends least privilege to AI systems including fine-grained model access control and training data access restrictions.

clauses.title: User Access Provisioning
clauses.rationale: AC-02 covers account management; IA-04/IA-05 address identifier and authenticator management.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to lifecycle management for AI service identities and model access credentials.

clauses.title: User Access Changes and Revocation
clauses.rationale: AC-02 covers account management; PS-04/PS-05 address personnel changes.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends deprovisioning to AI access including model repository access and training infrastructure permissions.

clauses.title: User Access Review
clauses.rationale: AC-02 covers account management; AC-06 addresses least privilege and access reviews.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends access reviews to AI system access including periodic review of model access permissions and data access rights.

clauses.title: Segregation of Privileged Access Roles
clauses.rationale: AC-05 covers separation of duties; AC-06 addresses least privilege.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends segregation to AI operations including separation between training, validation, and production environments.

clauses.title: Management of Privileged Access Roles
clauses.rationale: AC-02 covers account management; AC-06 addresses least privilege; IA-02 covers identification and authentication.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends privileged access to AI systems including admin access to ML platforms and model management systems.

clauses.title: Customers' Approval for Agreed Privileged Access Roles

clauses.gaps: AICM extends access authorization to AI-specific resources including training compute, model endpoints, and data pipelines.

clauses.title: Safeguard Logs Integrity
clauses.rationale: AU-09 covers protection of audit information; AU-10 addresses non-repudiation.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends user accountability to AI operations including audit trails for model modifications and training data changes.

clauses.title: Uniquely Identifiable Users

clauses.gaps: AICM extends user identification to AI platform users including federated identity for multi-cloud AI environments.

clauses.title: Strong Authentication
clauses.rationale: IA-02/IA-05/IA-08 comprehensively cover authentication, authenticator management, and cross-org identification.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends strong authentication to AI systems including MFA for model deployment and training infrastructure access.

clauses.title: Passwords and Secrets Management

clauses.gaps: AICM extends password management to AI system credentials including API keys, model access tokens, and service account credentials.

clauses.title: Authorization Mechanisms

clauses.gaps: AICM extends authorization to AI-specific resources including fine-grained model access control and data access governance.

clauses.title: Knowledge Access Control - Need to Know
clauses.rationale: AC-02 covers account management; IA-02 covers authentication; SC-07 provides boundary protection for AI API access.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses AI API authentication and authorization including model inference API security, rate limiting, and API key management for AI services.

clauses.title: Output Modification and Special Authorization

clauses.gaps: AICM addresses machine-to-machine identity for AI systems including model-to-model authentication, pipeline service identities, and automated AI agent identity management.

clauses.title: Agent Access Restriction
clauses.rationale: AC-05 covers separation of duties; AC-06 addresses least privilege; CM-05 restricts changes.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires role-based access control specific to AI lifecycle including separate roles for data scientists, ML engineers, model validators, and AI operations staff.

clauses.title: Interoperability and Portability Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: SA-01 covers acquisition policy; SA-04 addresses acquisition process and interoperability requirements.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends interoperability to AI systems including model portability, ML framework interoperability, and AI service migration.

clauses.title: Application Interface Availability
clauses.rationale: SA-04 covers acquisition processes; SA-09 addresses external services.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data portability to AI including training data export, model format standards (ONNX, PMML), and inference compatibility.

clauses.title: Secure Interoperability and Portability Management
clauses.rationale: SA-04 covers acquisition; SA-09 addresses external services; SC-08 covers transmission security.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends policy compatibility to AI services including API compatibility, model serving standards, and AI service level agreements.

clauses.title: Data Portability Contractual Obligations

clauses.gaps: AICM extends portability to AI-specific scenarios including multi-cloud ML deployment and vendor-neutral AI infrastructure.

clauses.title: Infrastructure and Virtualization Security Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: SA-01 covers acquisition policy; SC-01 covers system protection policy; CM-01 addresses configuration management.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends infrastructure security to AI-specific infrastructure including GPU clusters, ML platforms, and model serving infrastructure.

clauses.title: Capacity and Resource Planning
clauses.rationale: CP-02 covers contingency planning; SC-05 addresses denial of service protection; SC-06 covers resource availability.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends capacity planning to AI compute including GPU/TPU allocation, training job scheduling, and inference scaling.

clauses.title: Network Security
clauses.rationale: AC-04 enforces information flow; SC-07 provides boundary protection; SC-08 covers transmission security.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends network security to AI infrastructure including training network isolation and model serving network segmentation.

clauses.title: OS Hardening and Base Controls
clauses.rationale: CM-02/CM-06 cover baseline configurations and settings; SI-02 addresses flaw remediation.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends OS hardening to AI infrastructure including ML framework patching and GPU driver security.

clauses.title: Production and Non-Production Environments
clauses.rationale: CM-02 covers baselines; CM-04 addresses impact analysis; SC-07 covers boundary protection.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends to AI infrastructure hardening including container security for model serving and Kubernetes security for ML platforms.

clauses.title: Segmentation and Segregation
clauses.rationale: AC-04 covers information flow; SC-03 addresses security function isolation; SC-07 covers boundary protection.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends segmentation to AI environments including training/inference isolation and model environment separation.

clauses.title: Migration to Hosted Environments
clauses.rationale: CM-03 covers change control; SA-03 addresses development lifecycle; SA-04 covers acquisition processes.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends migration security to AI workloads including model migration, training data transfer, and AI environment portability.

clauses.title: Network Architecture Documentation
clauses.rationale: CA-09 covers internal connections; PL-02 addresses security concepts; SC-07 covers boundary protection.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends network architecture to AI infrastructure including dedicated AI network segments and high-bandwidth training interconnects.

clauses.title: Network Defense
clauses.rationale: SC-05 covers denial of service protection; SC-07 provides boundary protection; SI-04 covers system monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends network monitoring to AI infrastructure including training traffic analysis and inference endpoint monitoring.

clauses.title: Logging and Monitoring Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: AU-01 establishes audit policy; AU-02 defines auditable events.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends logging to AI-specific events including model inference logs, training events, and AI system decision logs.

clauses.title: Audit Logs Protection
clauses.rationale: AU-09 covers audit information protection; AU-11 addresses audit record retention.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends log protection to AI audit trails including model decision logs and training provenance records.

clauses.title: Security Monitoring and Alerting
clauses.rationale: AU-06 covers audit review; CA-07 provides continuous monitoring; SI-04 covers system monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends security monitoring to AI systems including model behavior monitoring and adversarial input detection.

clauses.title: Audit Logs Access and Accountability
clauses.rationale: AC-06 covers least privilege; AU-06/AU-09 address audit review and protection.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends audit access controls to AI logs including restricted access to model decision explanations.

clauses.title: Audit Logs Monitoring and Response
clauses.rationale: AU-06 covers audit analysis; IR-04 addresses incident handling; SI-04 covers monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends audit procedures to AI-specific analysis including AI anomaly investigation and model drift forensics.

clauses.title: Clock Synchronization
clauses.rationale: AU-08 directly addresses time stamps and clock synchronization for audit records.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends time synchronization to distributed AI systems including training cluster clocks and inference endpoint timestamps.

clauses.title: Logging Scope
clauses.rationale: AU-02 defines auditable events; AU-03 specifies audit record content.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends logging content to AI-specific data including model version, input features, confidence scores, and decision explanations.

clauses.title: Log Records
clauses.rationale: AU-02/AU-03 cover audit event types and record content for log sanitization requirements.
clauses.gaps: AICM addresses log sanitization for AI systems including removal of sensitive training data from logs and PII scrubbing from inference logs.

clauses.title: Log Protection
clauses.rationale: AU-09 protects audit information; AU-11 addresses retention.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends log storage to AI-specific retention requirements including model decision log retention for regulatory compliance.

clauses.title: Encryption Monitoring and Reporting
clauses.rationale: AU-02 defines events; CA-07 provides continuous monitoring; SC-13 covers cryptographic protection of logs.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends encryption monitoring to AI system logs including protection of model inference logs and training event records.

clauses.title: Transaction/Activity Logging
clauses.rationale: AU-02/AU-03/AU-12 comprehensively cover event definition, content, and generation.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends key event logging to AI-specific events including model deployment, retraining triggers, and bias threshold alerts.

clauses.title: Access Control Logs
clauses.rationale: AC-02 covers account management; AU-02/AU-03 cover audit events and content.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends access logging to AI systems including model access, training data access, and AI API usage tracking.

clauses.title: Failures and Anomalies Reporting
clauses.rationale: AU-05 covers audit processing failures; AU-06 addresses audit review; SI-04 covers monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends failures and anomalies monitoring to AI systems including training failures, model serving errors, and inference anomalies.

clauses.title: Input Monitoring
clauses.rationale: AU-02 covers audit events; AU-06 addresses audit review; SI-04 covers system monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI decision logging including model inference decision trails, feature importance logs, and explanation records for automated decisions. NIST does not address AI decision transparency.

clauses.title: Output Monitoring
clauses.rationale: AU-02/AU-03 cover audit events and content; CA-07 provides continuous monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model performance monitoring logs including drift detection metrics, accuracy degradation alerts, and model health dashboards.

clauses.title: Training Pipeline Security
clauses.rationale: SA-08 covers security engineering principles; CM-01 establishes configuration management; PL-01 covers planning. These provide general framework for model security governance.
clauses.gaps: AICM MDS-01 establishes comprehensive model security policy and procedures including model lifecycle governance, model risk management, and model security standards. NIST 800-53 has no equivalent model security controls.

clauses.title: Model Artifact Scanning
clauses.rationale: CM-08 covers component inventory; SA-03 addresses development lifecycle. These partially support model inventory.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model inventory and registry including model cards, versioning, lineage tracking, and model deprecation management. NIST has no model registry equivalent.

clauses.title: Model Documentation
clauses.rationale: SA-11 covers developer testing; CA-08 addresses penetration testing; RA-05 covers vulnerability scanning. These provide a testing framework adaptable to models.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model validation and testing including accuracy validation, bias testing, robustness testing, and adversarial evaluation. NIST testing controls are not designed for ML model validation.

clauses.title: Model Documentation Requirements
clauses.rationale: CM-03/CM-05 cover change control and access restrictions; SA-03 addresses development lifecycle.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model deployment security including deployment approval workflows, canary deployments, model signing, and deployment rollback procedures.

clauses.title: Model Documentation Validation
clauses.rationale: SI-04/CA-07 cover monitoring; AU-06 addresses audit review.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model monitoring including performance drift detection, data drift monitoring, concept drift alerts, and model behavior anomaly detection. NIST monitoring controls are infrastructure-focused.

clauses.title: Adversarial Attack Analysis
clauses.rationale: SI-07 covers integrity verification; SC-13 addresses cryptographic protection; CM-03 covers change control.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model integrity protection including cryptographic model signing, weight tampering detection, and model provenance verification.

clauses.title: Robustness against Adversarial Attack  / Model Hardening
clauses.rationale: AC-03/AC-06 cover access enforcement and least privilege; CM-05 restricts changes.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model access control including inference API authorization, model weight access restrictions, and model management role separation.

clauses.title: Model Integrity Checks
clauses.rationale: SA-11/RA-05 cover testing and vulnerability assessment; SI-07 covers integrity.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires adversarial robustness including adversarial attack testing, evasion resistance, and model hardening against adversarial inputs. NIST has no adversarial ML controls.

clauses.title: Model Signing/Ownership Verification
clauses.rationale: PT-01/PT-03 cover PII processing and minimization; SA-08 covers security engineering.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model privacy including membership inference protection, model inversion defense, differential privacy integration, and training data extraction prevention.

clauses.title: Model Continuous Monitoring
clauses.rationale: SA-03/SA-08/SA-15 cover development lifecycle, security engineering, and development standards.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires explainability and interpretability requirements including feature importance, decision explanation, and model transparency standards.

clauses.title: Model Failure
clauses.rationale: CM-03/CM-09 cover change control and configuration plans; SA-03 addresses development lifecycle.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model versioning and lifecycle management including model retirement, version compatibility, and model lineage tracking across training iterations.

clauses.title: Open Model Risk Assessment
clauses.rationale: SA-04/SA-09 cover acquisition and external services; RA-03 addresses risk assessment.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires third-party model governance including pre-trained model validation, foundation model risk assessment, and model supply chain security.

clauses.title: Secure Model Format
clauses.rationale: RA-03/PM-09 cover risk assessment and strategy; SI-01 addresses integrity policy.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires model risk quantification including model risk scoring, risk-based model tiering, and model failure impact assessment. NIST risk assessment is not designed for ML model risk.

clauses.title: Security Incident Management Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: IR-01 establishes incident response policy; IR-08 covers incident response plan.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends incident management to AI-specific incidents including model compromise, adversarial attacks, and AI system failures.

clauses.title: Service Management Policy and Procedures
clauses.rationale: IR-01/IR-04 cover incident response policy and handling; PM-01 establishes program.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends incident management to AI-specific roles and procedures.

clauses.title: Incident Response Plans

clauses.gaps: AICM extends incident response to AI-specific scenarios including model poisoning incidents and adversarial attack playbooks.

clauses.title: Incident Response Testing
clauses.rationale: IR-03 directly addresses incident response testing.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends testing to AI-specific incident scenarios including model failure drills and adversarial response exercises.

clauses.title: Incident Response Metrics

clauses.gaps: AICM extends metrics to AI-specific incident KPIs including model recovery time and AI attack detection rate.

clauses.title: Event Triage Processes

clauses.gaps: AICM extends evidence collection to AI-specific forensics including model state snapshots and inference log analysis.

clauses.title: Security Breach Notification
clauses.rationale: IR-06 covers incident reporting; IR-07 addresses incident response assistance.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends incident communication to AI-specific stakeholders including AI safety boards and regulatory bodies.

clauses.title: Points of Contact Maintenance

clauses.gaps: AICM extends external coordination to AI-specific organizations including AI safety organizations and industry AI security groups.

clauses.title: Incident Response
clauses.rationale: IR-01/IR-04/IR-06 cover incident response policy, handling, and reporting.
clauses.gaps: AICM requires AI-specific incident response procedures including adversarial attack playbooks, model compromise response, training data breach protocols, and AI system failure escalation paths.

clauses.gaps: AICM extends software restrictions to AI tools including ML framework installation policies and AI development tool management.

clauses.title: Compatibility
clauses.rationale: CM-02 covers baseline configurations; SA-04 addresses acquisition processes.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends compatibility to AI software including ML framework version management and GPU driver compatibility.

clauses.title: Endpoint Inventory
clauses.rationale: CM-08 directly addresses information system component inventory.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends inventory to AI-specific endpoints including GPU workstations, edge AI devices, and AI development environments.

clauses.title: Endpoint Management
clauses.rationale: CM-02/CM-03/CM-06 comprehensively cover baselines, change control, and configuration settings.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends configuration management to AI endpoint configurations including ML framework settings and AI development environment standards.

clauses.title: Automatic Lock Screen
clauses.rationale: AC-11 directly addresses session lock and screen lock functionality.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends device lock to AI workstations with access to sensitive training data and models.

clauses.title: Operating Systems
clauses.rationale: CM-02/CM-06 cover baselines and settings; SI-02 addresses flaw remediation.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends OS security to AI endpoint operating systems including ML framework security patches.

clauses.title: Storage Encryption
clauses.rationale: SC-13/SC-28 cover cryptographic protection and protection at rest.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends storage encryption to AI data at rest including local model caches and training data on endpoints.

clauses.title: Anti-Malware Detection and Prevention
clauses.rationale: SI-03 directly addresses malicious code protection including anti-malware.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends anti-malware to AI-specific threats on endpoints including adversarial sample detection.

clauses.title: Software Firewall
clauses.rationale: CM-07 covers least functionality; SC-07 provides boundary protection including firewall.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends software firewalls to AI endpoint network protection.

clauses.title: Data Loss Prevention
clauses.rationale: AC-04 covers information flow; SC-07 provides boundary protection; SI-04 covers monitoring.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends data loss prevention to AI data including preventing unauthorized model exfiltration and training data leakage.

clauses.title: Remote Locate
clauses.rationale: CM-08 covers component inventory for remote access devices.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends remote device management to AI development devices used remotely.

clauses.title: Remote Wipe
clauses.rationale: AC-19 covers mobile device access control; MP-06 addresses media sanitization.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends BYOD/mobile to AI workload access from personal devices.

clauses.title: Third-Party Endpoint Security Posture
clauses.rationale: AC-20 covers external system use; SA-09/SR-01 address external services and supply chain.
clauses.gaps: AICM extends third-party endpoint management to AI vendor devices and contractor AI development environments.
